sections.paragraph:
ὁμόφυλος, ον (ὁμός ‘common’, φῦλον ‘tribe’, cp. φυλή; Eur., Pla. X. et al.; pap; Jos., Ant. 17, 313) pert. to belonging to the same tribe, fellow-tribesman, compatriot subst. ὁ ὁμ. (Eth. Epic. col. 20, 19; Alciphron 1, 10, 5; 2 Macc 4:10; 3 Macc 3:21; Philo; Jos., Ant. 17, 285; Just., A I, 14, 3) 1 Cl 4:10.—DELG s.v. φῦλον.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

Epic. Epic. = Epicus, of an epic poet, s. Epici

col. col. = column

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 710
